channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: XO Team Members: Profiles and Key Information
item.description: The article provides an in-depth look at the XO Team, a collective of social media influencers and creators based in Eastern Europe. Founded in 2020 by Gary Grey and Mary Senn, the team has grown to i...

item.title: BetPawa extends sponsorship to boost Dar es Salaam basketball league
item.description: Sports betting firm betPawa has renewed its sponsorship of the Basketball Dar es Salaam League (BDL), allocating Sh588.9 million for the 2026 season. The Locker Room Bonus (LRB) program will reward pl...

item.title: Urbanisation reshapes frog communities in Western Ghats, study finds
item.description: A study conducted in Udupi, Karnataka, reveals that urbanisation in the Western Ghats is not just causing species loss but reshaping frog communities by filtering species based on traits like body siz...

item.title: Five Labourers Killed in Balochistan Attack
item.description: Five labourers were killed in a targeted attack in Balochistan's Washuk district, where armed men opened fire on workers at a shop in the Mashkel area. Police confirmed the victims, who had travelled ...

item.title: Atiku Warns Fee Hikes May Push Children Out Of School
item.description: Presidential candidate Atiku Abubakar has criticized the recent increase in fees for Federal Unity Colleges and the approval of a uniform ₦50,000 examination fee for WAEC and NECO candidates from 2027...

item.title: Toronto Salsa Festival Cancelled Following Fatal Shooting Incident
item.description: The Toronto Salsa on St. Clair festival was abruptly cancelled after a fatal shooting incident occurred near the city's waterfront. Police are investigating the incident, which resulted in five people...

item.title: ACSA implements enhanced safety protocols at Cape Town Airport to address fake Uber driver incidents
item.description: Airports Company South Africa (ACSA) has intensified safety measures at Cape Town International Airport (CTIA) following reports of fake Uber drivers overcharging passengers. The initiative follows a ...

item.title: Cartoon Highlights from July 13, 2026
item.description: This article compiles the top political cartoons published on July 13, 2026, by The Age. The piece features three visually striking illustrations that comment on contemporary Australian political issu...

item.title: Weekend Pop-Up Markets in Pampanga Boost Local Food and Business Exposure
item.description: Weekend pop-up markets in Pampanga have become vibrant hubs where local merchants, food entrepreneurs, and visitors gather to explore diverse culinary offerings. These markets, now regular attractions...

item.title: Pune Building Collapse: 9 Lives Lost as Rescue Efforts Conclude
item.description: A waste-to-energy plant's administrative building in Pune collapsed on July 8, trapping 23 employees. Rescue teams recovered the last missing person, Waman Kasbe, on Sunday, raising the death toll to ...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Helps Neighbour's Daughter Fulfil Late Mother's Dream of Working Abroad
item.description: A Kenyan woman, Chirie Masai, is appealing for help to secure employment abroad for her late neighbour's daughter, Lydia Chirie Masai. Lydia, a trained teacher, is determined to support her nine young...

item.title: Bishops Diocesan College debates Pride flag display amid community tensions
item.description: Bishops Diocesan College in Cape Town has sparked significant debate after its Old Diocesan Committee (ODC) demanded the removal of the Pride flag from school premises. The committee argued that only ...

item.title: Grandparents Shoulder Responsibility for Grandchildren Amid Parental Neglect in Tanzania
item.description: Elderly caregivers across Tanzania are increasingly taking on the responsibility of raising grandchildren as biological parents neglect their duties, creating significant social, economic, and emotion...

item.title: New Standards for Universities to Combat Antisemitism and Racism
item.description: The Albanese government has introduced new mandatory standards requiring Australian universities to implement robust measures against racism and antisemitism, effective from Monday. Vice chancellors w...

item.title: US Congressman Ro Khanna alleges armed Israeli settlers detained his delegation during West Bank visit
item.description: US Congressman Ro Khanna says he and several other Americans were detained for about 90 minutes by armed Israeli settlers while visiting the occupied West Bank. According to Khanna, the settlers, carr...

item.title: Bank of Canada maintains interest rates amid economic stability and inflation control
item.description: The Bank of Canada is anticipated to keep interest rates unchanged at 2.25% for the sixth consecutive time, as lower oil prices and improved economic data ease previous policy tensions. Since February...

item.title: Iran and US escalate conflict over Strait of Hormuz control
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between Iran and the US over control of the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides launching strikes and accusing each other of aggression. Iran claims to have c...

item.title: El Niño's Impact: South Africa Faces Extreme Heat and Drought Risks
item.description: A powerful El Niño climate pattern is rapidly developing in the Pacific Ocean, with a 90% chance of persisting until November. This phenomenon will likely bring extreme heat, drought, and veld fires t...

item.title: Philippine Sports Commission plans to expand tennis infrastructure and competitions
item.description: The Philippine Sports Commission (PSC) is planning to increase the number of tennis courts and organize more high-level tournaments to ensure the continued growth of tennis in the country. This initia...

item.title: Successful arrest of Ansaru terrorists after Oyo schoolchildren rescue
item.description: A viral video has captured the moment security operatives arrested suspected Ansaru terrorists involved in the abduction of over 50 schoolchildren and teachers from Oyo State. The victims, held captiv...
